nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated. Generally reported from muddy and sandy bottoms adjacent to rocky structure and patch reefs, in coastal lagoons, seagrass beds, and inshore reef edges. Reported depth range 0–30 m (most records 1–15 m). Tends to shelter cryptically among seagrass, rubble, and rockwork during the day and is reported to use the spines of long-spined sea urchins as cover, foraging more actively at dusk and through the night.

diet.notes: Generally accepts a varied carnivore diet of mysid shrimp, vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, finely chopped marine flesh, krill, copepods, amphipods, and quality prepared frozen carnivore foods. Pellet and flake foods tend to be accepted once specimens are settled, though newly imported individuals may initially refuse dry foods. Multiple small feedings per day are generally advised, and dusk or evening feeding has been reported to improve acceptance given the species' nocturnal foraging tendency.

summary: A small, cryptically patterned Indo-Pacific cardinalfish sold under the LiveAquaria trade name 'Speckled Fowler Cardinalfish' (Fowleria sp., best matched to F. isostigma). Generally peaceful and reef-safe; tends to shelter in seagrass, rubble, or among urchin spines.
careNotes:
The Speckled Fowler Cardinalfish is a small Indo-Pacific apogonid in the genus Fowleria, typically reaching around 7–9 cm in length. LiveAquaria sells it as 'Fowleria sp.' without committing to a specific epithet. Based on the LiveAquaria description (black, tan, and green camouflage coloration, brown and black markings, slow methodical swimmer, hides in seagrass or among long-spined urchin spines, with a black spot behind the gill plate that has been suggested to function as predator-deterrent eye mimicry) and on the secondary source URL supplied for this research run, the most consistent identification is Fowleria isostigma (the Dotted Cardinalfish), which FishBase describes as brown to tan with small black spots on the scales, inhabiting muddy bottoms and reef-associated areas at 0–30 m across the tropical Indo-Pacific.

The identification is NOT fully certain. The genus contains several visually similar small cryptic cardinalfishes (notably F. variegata, the Variegated / Spotted / Peppered Cardinalfish, which reaches similar size and has overlapping range), and LiveAquaria's parallel listing of an 'Fowleria Cardinalfish' explicitly labelled as F. isostigma is described with orange-to-red coloration and a red or yellow tail — quite distinct from the dark camouflage form being researched here. This entry is therefore flagged as needs_review pending visual confirmation against an authoritative photo of the species being shipped under this trade name.

In the aquarium it tends to do best when given ample live rock with caves, rubble, seagrass or plant cover, and dim shaded retreats. It is generally reported as reef-safe and has not been documented to nip corals or sessile invertebrates; very small ornamental shrimp may occasionally be eaten as with most marine carnivores. Sources consistently describe a peaceful, social temperament — small groups can be kept together and tend to establish a loose hierarchy without overt aggression, though Tank Facts notes occasional territoriality toward closely-related conspecifics in tight quarters.

The species is largely nocturnal in its feeding habits. Newly imported specimens may initially refuse prepared foods; live or vitamin-enriched frozen foods offered at dusk are generally helpful for getting individuals feeding. Once settled, the species is reported as durable provided water quality and feeding are consistent.

Cross-source notes: LiveAquaria gives a minimum tank size of 30 US gallons (~113 L) for a 3-inch fish and rates care level as 'easy'; Reeflex recommends ~150 L (33 gal) and rates difficulty as 'average'; Tank Facts gives a slightly larger adult size (3.5 in / ~9 cm) without a tank size. The 150 L value written here biases toward the larger end (per playbook caution) and matches Reeflex's recommendation; for a small shoal this is a sensible minimum and provides swimming room around structured rockwork. Adult size has been averaged across sources to 7–9 cm (FishBase max 7 cm SL; Reeflex 7 cm TL; Tank Facts 9 cm). Temperature range was averaged across LiveAquaria (22.2–25.6°C), Tank Facts (24.4–26.7°C), Reeflex (24–27°C), and FishBase wild-water modelling (25.8–29.3°C); the written 23–28°C captures the typical aquarium spectrum without adopting the FishBase environmental-modelling upper bound, which reflects sea-surface temperatures rather than recommended captive husbandry.

Also sold by LiveAquaria under the plain trade name 'Fowleria Cardinalfish' (and sometimes 'Redtail Cardinalfish'), described on that listing as displaying orange-to-red body coloration with a red or yellow tail — a noticeably different color form from the dark tan-and-black cryptic patterning of the 'Speckled Fowler' listing. Whether these represent distinct populations, sexual dimorphism, ontogenetic color change, or a separate Fowleria species being traded under the same scientific name is not resolved in available sources; both trade names are therefore recorded in alsoKnownAs and this entry remains flagged needs_review pending expert visual verification.
breedingNotes: Like other cardinalfishes, members of the genus Fowleria are paternal mouthbrooders with distinct pair formation during courtship and spawning. After spawning the male is generally reported to carry the egg mass in his buccal cavity for roughly 1–3 weeks and to largely cease feeding while brooding; a visibly swollen jaw is the most obvious sign. Targeted captive-breeding records for Fowleria isostigma are sparse compared to better-studied genera like Pterapogon and Sphaeramia; success in a community tank is unlikely without a dedicated rearing setup.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary husbandry source. Gave trade name 'Speckled Fowler Cardinalfish', scientific name as 'Fowleria sp.' (no epithet committed), origin Cebu/Indonesia, max size 3 in (~7.6 cm), minimum tank 30 gal (~113 L), care level easy, temperament peaceful, reef-safe yes, diet (meaty foods: feeder shrimp, flake, pellet, marine flesh, bloodworms), water parameters (72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12), color form (black/tan/green with camouflage markings, black spot behind gill plate), behaviour (slow methodical swimmer, hides in seagrass or among long-spined urchin spines, small groups establish hierarchy without aggression).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Designated secondary source. The mnhn.fr mirror returned HTTP 403; data was obtained from the fishbase.se mirror for the same species page. Gave taxonomy (Apogonidae, Kurtiformes), authorship (Jordan & Seale, 1906), max size 7.0 cm SL, depth range 0–30 m, Indo-Pacific distribution (35°N–20°S, 109°E–179°E), habitat (muddy bottoms and reef-associated, found near rocky areas), wild temperature 25.8–29.3°C (mean 28.7°C, environmental modelling), reproduction mode (distinct pairing during courtship and spawning), and IUCN status (Least Concern, assessed 2023-08-14). Coloration: brown to tan body with small black spots on scales — consistent with the LiveAquaria 'Speckled Fowler' camouflage description.

additional.name: Reeflex
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (7 cm / 2.76 in), care level (average), minimum tank size (33 gal / ~150 L), temperature 24–27°C, depth 0–30 m, diet (brine shrimp, flakes, mysis, zooplankton), distribution (Indo-Pacific including Australia, Coral Sea, Philippines, Japan, PNG, Micronesia).

additional.name: Tank Facts
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (3.5 in / ~9 cm), temperament (peaceful, social, may become territorial toward conspecifics in tight quarters), diet (frozen and live brine, mysis, krill, marine pellet/flake), water parameters (76–80°F / ~24.4–26.7°C, pH 7.8–8.4, SG 1.023–1.026, dKH 8–12), and behaviour (nocturnal, generally hardy).

additional.name: Fishes of Australia
additional.notes: Cross-checked Australian distribution (Cocos Keeling, Ashmore & Cartier Islands, Scott Reef, Darwin, Cape York to Capricorn Group / Great Barrier Reef), habitat (reefs, muddy bottoms), max size 7 cm DL, depth 1–15 m. Confirms the Indo-Pacific reef-associated muddy-bottom habitat described elsewhere.

fish.breedingNotes: Paternal mouthbrooder typical of family Apogonidae; pair forms during courtship and spawning. Male is generally reported to carry the egg mass in the buccal cavity for roughly 1–3 weeks and to largely stop feeding while brooding. Targeted captive-breeding records for Fowleria isostigma are sparse; community-tank larval survival is unlikely without a dedicated rearing setup.
